content.thinking: The review comment says: `ConversationBuffer` in `internal/agent/history.go` will panic with division by zero if capacity is 0. Need to handle the `0` case safely.

content.text: The review comment flags a division-by-zero panic in `ConversationBuffer` when `history_size` is set to `0`. Let me look at the code.
Now let me check how `NewConversationBuffer` is called and if there are existing tests.

content.text: The fix: when capacity is 0, `Add` should be a no-op and `Turns` should return empty. I'll guard in the methods rather than in the constructor, so a zero-capacity buffer simply disables history as documented.

content.content: The file /tmp/klaus-sessions/reel-life/20260404-1627-0824/internal/agent/history.go has been updated successfully.

lines: // Add appends a turn, overwriting the oldest when full.
+// If the buffer capacity is 0, the call is a no-op (history disabled).
// Buffer full — overwrite oldest and advance head.

content.text: Now let me add a test for the zero-capacity case.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: handle zero-capacity ConversationBuffer to prevent division-by-zero panic

When history_size is set to 0 (disabled), Add() now returns early instead
of panicking on modulo-zero. Adds test for the zero-capacity case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
